steps.title: Separate independent clauses
steps.content: The sentence contains two independent ideas: Rudolph did not just walk, she ran fast; and during the 1960 Summer Olympics, she won multiple races. A comma alone cannot properly join those clauses.

steps.title: Use a clear sentence break
steps.content: Choice A uses confusing dash punctuation and still leaves a comma before the next independent clause. Choice B fuses the clauses with only a comma after the introductory phrase. Choice C creates a comma splice. The period cleanly ends the first sentence before the Olympics detail begins. The answer is D.
